trajectory_summary: The call builds genuine energy through the verify section — Guillermo is an active, informed shopper with real bill pain and a clear purchase preference — then leaks that energy in the back half as Pike handles the in-person format resistance in three separate passes without ever naming the root concern. The appointment is booked but structurally fragile: the wife is not anchored as a required attendee, Guillermo's phone-call preference was not fully resolved, and a financing claim was made that the closer will need to either honor or walk back.
outcome_dispute: The v1 tag is unknown. The observable outcome is a booked in-person appointment for Tuesday at 5PM. However, the appointment carries conditional fragility: Guillermo's wife is on title but not explicitly confirmed as attending, Guillermo expressed a preference for a phone call that was not resolved to his satisfaction, and a blanket no-out-of-pocket financing claim was made without closer-tier authority. This should be tagged as a conditional booking, not a clean sit.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: my wife and I, we have eight automated schedules, so it's hard to get us both at the same time, in at the house.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Pike confirmed the wife is on title at 04:23 and then booked the appointment at 08:48 without confirming she will attend. Guillermo explicitly flagged the scheduling difficulty of getting both of them together — the protocol trigger was live and named. A one-leg appointment with a co-owner absent is structurally vulnerable at the close. This is the highest-priority coaching beat because it is both a protocol gap and a direct threat to the sit quality.

key_moments.quote: So are you a, how you call it, PPA or or you sell the system?
key_moments.stewart_read: This is Guillermo's first competitive-context signal and an early trigger for verify.prior_designs. He is not just asking about Ion's product — he is revealing he has already been through the PPA-vs-purchase conversation with other companies and is running a filter. Pike correctly defers the product question to the closer, but he does not use this moment to probe competitive context: how many companies has Guillermo seen, what did they offer, what did he like or dislike? That intelligence would sharpen the closer's opening frame.

key_moments.quote: I'm not interested in the PPA at all. That's that's just like, you can put that in the notes as well.
key_moments.stewart_read: Guillermo draws a hard line and explicitly asks for it to be noted. Pike does not verbally confirm the note was taken, does not reassure Guillermo that purchase/loan options exist, and moves on. The customer has just handed Pike a trust-building moment — 'I hear you, I've got that down, and I want you to know our specialist works with purchase options' — and Pike lets it pass. This is reactive: there is no evidence Pike is deferring strategically; he simply moves to the next question.

key_moments.quote: the delivery fees are the ones that are, you know, a little bit upsetting.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the call's clearest flip opportunity. Guillermo has just articulated genuine bill pain — not the kWh number, but the delivery-fee grievance — and illustrated it with the Ticketmaster analogy at 03:25. Per coaching_philosophy.objection_inversion, the move here is to flip: 'That's exactly what solar addresses — you stop paying Pepco's delivery markup and pay for what you actually generate.' Pike instead says 'of course' and moves to the roof question. The emotional peak of the call is not anchored.

key_moments.quote: no matter what you're looking at, that I do know that for sure. There is not a pocket cost or any of these no matter what you look at with us.
key_moments.stewart_read: At 02:00 Pike explicitly disclaimed knowledge of PPA vs. loan specifics. Here, 90 seconds later, he makes a blanket financing assertion — no out-of-pocket cost on any option — as a reassurance move. This is scope creep: Pike is making a closer-tier claim without closer-tier authority. The claim may be accurate for some options and not others. If the closer walks in and the no-out-of-pocket framing does not apply to the purchase option Guillermo wants, the appointment starts with a trust deficit.
